title: Understanding Torque
text: To find the angular acceleration, we need to calculate the net torque acting on the grindstone. Torque is given by the formula: Torque $$=$$ Force * Radius. Consider both the applied force and the frictional force.

title: Calculate Torque from Applied Force
text: The torque due to the applied force is calculated using the formula: Torque $$=$$ Force * Radius. Use the given values: Force $$=$$ $$180$$ N and Radius $$=$$ $$0.280$$ $$m$$.

title: Torque from Applied Force
text: Calculate the torque from the applied force using the formula: Torque $$=$$ Force * Radius.

title: Calculate Torque from Frictional Force
text: The torque due to the frictional force is calculated using the formula: Torque $$=$$ Force * Radius. Use the given values: Force $$=$$ $$20.0$$ N and Radius $$=$$ $$0.015$$ $$m$$.

title: Torque from Frictional Force
text: Calculate the torque from the frictional force using the formula: Torque $$=$$ Force * Radius.

title: Net Torque Calculation
text: The net torque is the difference between the torque from the applied force and the torque from the frictional force. Use the formula: Net Torque $$=$$ $$Torque_{applied}$$ - $$Torque_{friction}$$.

title: Net Torque
text: Calculate the net torque using the formula: Net Torque $$=$$ $$Torque_{applied}$$ - $$Torque_{friction}$$.

title: Moment of Inertia
text: The moment of inertia for a solid disk is given by the formula: I $$=$$ $$\frac{1}{2}$$ * M * $$R^2$$. Use the given values: M $$=$$ $$75.0$$ kg and R $$=$$ $$0.280$$ $$m$$.

title: Calculate Moment of Inertia
text: Calculate the moment of inertia using the formula: I $$=$$ $$\frac{1}{2}$$ * M * $$R^2$$.

title: Angular Acceleration Formula
text: Angular acceleration is calculated using the formula: Angular Acceleration $$=$$ Net Torque / Moment of Inertia.

title: Calculate Angular Acceleration
text: Calculate the angular acceleration using the formula: Angular Acceleration $$=$$ Net Torque / Moment of Inertia.
